root_text.an4.97:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya?
root_text.an4.97:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo khippanisantī ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu, sutānañca dhammānaṁ dhārakajātiko hoti, dhātānañca dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhī hoti atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya, dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an4.97:2.3: no ca kalyāṇavāco hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā, no ca sandassako hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an4.97:2.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya.
root_text.an4.97:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo parahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no attahitāya?
root_text.an4.97:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na heva kho khippanisantī hoti kusalesu dhammesu, no ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhārakajātiko hoti, no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhī hoti, no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an4.97:3.3: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā, sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an4.97:3.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo parahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no attahitāya.
root_text.an4.97:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo nevattahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya?
root_text.an4.97:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na heva kho khippanisantī hoti kusalesu dhammesu, no ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhārakajātiko hoti, no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhī hoti, no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an4.97:4.3: no ca kalyāṇavāco hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā, no ca sandassako hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an4.97:4.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo nevattahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya.
root_text.an4.97:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya ceva paṭipanno hoti parahitāya ca?
root_text.an4.97:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo khippanisantī ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu, sutānañca dhammānaṁ dhārakajātiko hoti, dhātānañca dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhī hoti atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya, dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an4.97:5.3: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā, sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an4.97:5.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya ceva paṭipanno hoti parahitāya ca.
root_text.an4.97:5.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.97:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.97:1.3: One who practices to benefit themselves, but not others;
translation_text.an4.97:1.4: one who practices to benefit others, but not themselves;
translation_text.an4.97:1.5: one who practices to benefit neither themselves nor others; and
translation_text.an4.97:1.6: one who practices to benefit both themselves and others.
translation_text.an4.97:2.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit themselves, but not others?
translation_text.an4.97:2.2: It’s when an individual is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings. They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard. They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized. Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an4.97:2.3: But they’re not a good speaker and do not enunciate well. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning. They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an4.97:2.4: That’s how an individual practices to benefit themselves, but not others.
translation_text.an4.97:3.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit others, but not themselves?
translation_text.an4.97:3.2: It’s when an individual is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings. …
translation_text.an4.97:3.4: That’s how an individual practices to benefit others, but not themselves.
translation_text.an4.97:4.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit neither themselves nor others?
translation_text.an4.97:4.2: It’s when an individual is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings. …
translation_text.an4.97:4.3: Nor are they a good speaker. …
translation_text.an4.97:4.4: That’s how an individual practices to benefit neither themselves nor others.
translation_text.an4.97:5.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit both themselves and others?
translation_text.an4.97:5.2: It’s when an individual is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings. …
translation_text.an4.97:5.4: That’s how an individual practices to benefit both themselves and others.
translation_text.an4.97:5.5: These are the four individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.97:2.2: dhārakajātiko → dhāraṇajātiko (mr) | dhātānañca → dhatānañca (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
